symptoms.symptom_name: Acute bilateral lower-limb weakness with inability to bear weight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New bilateral motor deficit with loss of ambulation is a cardinal red flag for cord/cauda compression.
Sensory level/loss accompanies motor deficit in an evolving cord lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Saddle numbness with impaired bladder/bowel sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Saddle anaesthesia and sphincter disturbance are specific features mandating emergency imaging and decompression.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe unremitting lumbar/pelvic bone pain, analgesia-refractory, over three weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive, night/rest, analgesia-refractory axial pain is a hallmark of bony metastatic disease.
Visible haematuria in an older man requires urgent work-up for urological cancer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss, anorexia, fatigue (constitutional decline)
Chronic lower urinary tract symptoms (weak stream, hesitancy, nocturia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: LUTS are non-specific but, with the other features, point to prostate involvement; cannot distinguish benign from malignant without examination and PSA.
Absence of fever with a consumptive history argues against an acute febrile urinary infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hard, nodular, irregular prostate fixed to surrounding tissue on DRE

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A fixed, hard, nodular gland is highly suggestive of prostate carcinoma — obtainable only on in-person DRE; sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lower-limb weakness 3/5 proximal with diminished deep tendon reflexes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective motor and reflex deficit confirms an evolving cord lesion; sealed clinician examination finding.
Bilateral leg oedema suggests pelvic nodal/venous obstruction; the patient may observe swelling but its cause requires examination.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Systemic decompensation / pain and constitutional stress
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Measured vitals are a sealed clinician observation; tachycardia is non-specific here (pain, distress, advanced illness).

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum prostate-specific antigen (PSA) markedly elevated (typically >100 ng/mL)

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Delayed staging of malignancy; not obtainable via telehealth and not required before emergency escalation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A PSA well above 100 ng/mL strongly indicates metastatic prostate cancer; hospital investigation, not available in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dense osteoblastic metastases on radionuclide bone scan

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Staging investigation performed at hospital; not required before emergency escalation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Osteoblastic bone metastases are characteristic of prostate primary; sealed hospital investigation.

investigation_details.expected_result: Metastatic epidural deposit compressing the cord/cauda equina
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The definitive emergency imaging; performed emergently at hospital — never obtainable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Emergency MRI confirms the level and extent of compression and directs decompression/radiotherapy.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Malignant spinal cord / cauda equina compression
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe axial pain plus new bilateral motor/sensory deficit and sphincter/saddle involvement is a time-critical emergency demanding immediate escalation and MRI.
Bone pain, haematuria, LUTS, constitutional decline, a hard fixed prostate, very high PSA and osteoblastic bone metastases together indicate advanced prostate cancer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent whole-spine MRI (at hospital, via emergency pathway)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive imaging for suspected cord compression; arranged emergently after 000 escalation, not by the telehealth service directly.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum PSA and staging bloods (at hospital)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and stages the underlying malignancy; part of the hospital work-up, not required before emergency transfer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Outpatient/community imaging or GP-arranged scan instead of emergency referral
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Diverting a patient with new bilateral leg weakness to outpatient imaging or a GP appointment is dangerous under-triage — this is an emergency.
